title: Psychotria paradoxalis
taxonomy: Psychotria paradoxalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Psychotria, which comprises approximately 1,800 species of tropical plants. Psychotria paradoxalis is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Rubiaceae family, also known as the coffee or madder family, is one of the largest families of flowering plants. This species is part of the Rubiaceae family due to its characteristic flowers and inflorescences.
uses: Psychotria paradoxalis has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties in various cultures.  However, information on its medicinal uses is limited.  Further research is needed to confirm its potential health benefits.  Some studies suggest that it may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.  More studies are required to fully understand the medicinal uses of Psychotria paradoxalis.
constituents: Unfortunately, I do not have the specific information about the active constituents of Psychotria paradoxalis.
